persona.name: Robert Garcia

occupation.title: Retired Firefighter (Captain)
occupation.description: Retired after 30 years of service, the last 8 as a station captain. Now volunteers two mornings a week at the fire museum and serves on the city's emergency preparedness advisory board. Fixed income from pension and small 401(k).

persona.education: Associate degree in Fire Science from Central New Mexico Community College. Fire Officer I and II certifications. Completed numerous FEMA and ICS courses over his career.
long_term_goals: Walk 5,000 miles in retirement (tracking on a spreadsheet)
Keep his weight under 200 lbs — cardiologist's target
Be around to see his grandkids graduate high school
Take his wife on a cruise to Mexico
persona.style: Robert is steady, straightforward, and practical in a way that comes from decades of emergency work. He says what he means without drama. He evaluates things through a durability-and-function lens — does it work, is it built well, will it hold up? He doesn't care about trends or brands. He's respectful and polite in conversation but doesn't waste time on small talk. He wears New Balance or whatever his wife picks up for him, jeans, and a retired firefighter ball cap. He's the kind of man who holds doors open and remembers your name.

traits: Steady and dependable — decades of crisis management shaped this
Follows medical advice carefully since his cardiac event
No vanity about products — function and comfort are everything
Patient and humble — doesn't need to prove himself anymore
Values community and family above personal ambition
Quietly disciplined about his daily walking routine

interests: Fire department history and museum volunteer work
likes: Shoes with firm arch support and cushioned heel
Brands he can find at a local store and try on
Velcro or easy-lace systems for ease
Products that last at least a year of daily use
Shoes under $100 that are comfortable from day one
dislikes: Shoes marketed to runners when he needs walking shoes
Narrow toe boxes that squeeze his wide feet
Online-only shoes he can't try on first
Being told he should 'start running' by well-meaning people

beliefs: Walking every day keeps him out of the hospital — his cardiologist said so and he believes it
Comfortable shoes are a medical necessity at his age, not a luxury
You take care of your feet the way you take care of your tools — they need to work tomorrow
He's not a runner and doesn't want to be — walking is enough
skills: Vehicle mechanics and classic car restoration

general: Walks 2 miles every morning along the Rio Grande bosque trail
Tracks daily steps with a simple pedometer — targets 6,000-8,000
Replaces shoes when his wife tells him the tread is gone
Buys shoes in-store at Big 5 Sporting Goods or Dillard's

morning: Walk 2 miles along the bosque — same route
Shower, check blood pressure, log it
workday: Advisory board meetings once a month
Garage project work — Bronco or woodworking
Afternoon walk with his wife (30 minutes)
evening: Dinner — usually his wife's cooking or he grills
Watch the news and Lobos games
Read Western novels or fire service history
weekend: Saturday: farmers market, then garage project
Walk with wife and sometimes grandkids
Sunday: church, then family lunch at daughter's house

health.physical: Had a mild heart attack (NSTEMI) 5 years ago. Two stents placed. On daily aspirin, statin, and beta-blocker. Blood pressure well-controlled with medication. Walks daily per cardiologist's instruction — no running allowed. Wide feet (12 EE) that have been in bunker boots for 30 years. Mild neuropathy in toes. Weight: 198 lbs, target is under 200.
health.mental: Adjusted well to retirement after initial restlessness. Finds purpose in volunteering and family. Some anxiety about cardiac health — checks blood pressure twice daily. Occasional sadness about colleagues lost in the line of duty. Talks to his wife about it but wouldn't see a therapist.

relationships.name: Maria Garcia
relationships.description: Retired school librarian. They've been married 38 years. She manages his medications and walks with him in the afternoons. The organizational force of the household.

relationships.name: Sofia Garcia-Torres
relationships.description: Elementary school teacher in Albuquerque. Two kids (8 and 5). Sunday family lunches are at her house.

relationships.name: Daniel Garcia
relationships.description: Active-duty firefighter in Phoenix. Robert is proud but anxious. They talk about fire service topics.

relationships.name: Leo and Emma
relationships.description: The reason Robert walks every day. He wants to see them grow up.

relationships.name: Dr. Patel
relationships.description: Robert follows his instructions precisely. Quarterly checkups. The doctor praised his walking consistency.
